unit: Unit 12: Everyday Life with Your Computer
title: Google Drive: Your Online Filing Cabinet
drDigitalIntro:
Google Drive is where all your online documents, photos, and files live — like a filing cabinet in the cloud.

Try it on your own computer:

1. Open your web browser.
2. Go to drive.google.com (sign in if asked).
3. Find the document you created in the last lesson — it's already there!
4. Now try uploading a file from your computer: click the + New button, then File upload.
5. Pick any file on your computer (a photo, a document, anything).
6. Watch it upload — once it's done, that file now lives in the cloud too.

You get 15 GB of free space — plenty for documents and photos. If you ever need more, Google will offer a paid plan.
drDigitalSuccess: You uploaded a file to Google Drive! Now it's backed up online and you can access it from any device with a web browser.
drDigitalHint: Go to drive.google.com, click + New, then File upload, and pick a file from your computer.
